name: Rings
description: Resonator built on three physical models: modal (bells/bars), sympathetic strings (Karplus network), and inharmonic strings. Excited by trigger button, internal noise, or external audio input.

controls.name: Resonator
controls.description: Three-position button cycles model: modal · sympathetic strings · inharmonic/FM string (stock firmware). Long-press toggles polyphony 1/2/4.
controls.detail: Orange = modal · green = sympathetic · red = inharmonic · LEDs also show polyphony count

controls.name: Polyphony
controls.description: Long-press of Resonator button cycles polyphony: 1 / 2 / 4 voices. Higher polyphony reduces per-voice DSP so longer decays shorten.
controls.detail: Note-stealing assigns new triggers to oldest voice · 1V/Oct tracks last played

controls.name: Frequency
controls.description: Coarse pitch of the resonator fundamental. Covers full audio range.
controls.detail: ±4 octaves around C3 · Attenuverter-free; pair with external offset for fine trim

controls.name: Structure
controls.description: Modal: inharmonicity of partials. Sympathetic: ratio between sympathetic strings. Inharmonic: stiffness/tension of the string.
controls.detail: Has bipolar attenuverter for STRUCTURE CV input · 0–1 normalized range

controls.name: Brightness
controls.description: Spectral tilt and excitation color. Low = dull mallet, high = bright/ringing.
controls.detail: Has bipolar attenuverter · also shapes internal noise exciter when IN unpatched

controls.name: Damping
controls.description: Decay time of resonant modes. CW extends decay toward infinite sustain/feedback.
controls.detail: Has bipolar attenuverter · at maximum + modal mode the resonator self-oscillates

controls.name: Position
controls.description: Pickup/strike position on the virtual body. Shifts which partials are emphasized.
controls.detail: Has bipolar attenuverter · sweeping produces comb-filter-like motion

inputs.name: V/OCT
inputs.description: 1V/oct pitch input tracking the resonator fundamental.

inputs.name: STRUCTURE CV
inputs.description: CV for Structure parameter, scaled by its attenuverter.

inputs.name: STRUM
inputs.description: Trigger input. Plucks/strikes the resonator and samples V/OCT for the new voice.
inputs.normalizedTo: rising-edge detector on V/OCT when unpatched

inputs.name: IN
inputs.description: Audio input that excites the resonator. When unpatched, an internal noise+mallet exciter is used and STRUM drives it.

outputs.name: ODD
outputs.description: Odd partials / one pickup of the resonator. Dry+wet mix when IN is patched.

outputs.name: EVEN
outputs.description: Even partials / opposite pickup. Complementary to ODD — sum for full signal, pan for stereo.

behaviors.name: Modal resonator
behaviors.description: Modal synthesis from 24–64 tuned partials. STRUCTURE inharmonicity, BRIGHTNESS damps higher modes, DAMPING sets decay. Excellent for metal bars, bells, glass, gongs.

behaviors.name: Sympathetic strings
behaviors.description: Network of Karplus-Strong strings with sympathetic resonance. STRUCTURE sets inter-string ratios (perfect fifth/octave/etc.). Produces sitar, koto, piano-like textures.

behaviors.name: Modal / non-linear string (Inharmonic)
behaviors.description: Stiff string with non-linear terminator. STRUCTURE controls stiffness: low = nylon pluck, high = bell-like FM string. High DAMPING produces self-oscillating FM drones.

behaviors.name: Easter-egg alternate models
behaviors.description: Holding Polyphony button while selecting mode exposes FM string, Western chord, and disastrous peace modes (stock firmware v1.2). Use to access drone, organ, and reverb behaviors not labelled on the panel.

behaviors.name: External excitation
behaviors.description: Any audio source (drum, voice, synth) excites the resonator. STRUM no longer re-strikes — the external signal continuously drives the modes. Breaks the internal mallet normalling.

behaviors.name: Self-triggering from V/OCT
behaviors.description: When STRUM is not patched Rings watches V/OCT for sudden voltage jumps and auto-triggers the exciter on pitch change. Enables playing from a sequencer with no separate gate cable.

behaviors.name: Self-oscillation / drone
behaviors.description: Maximum damping brings the resonator to infinite decay so it sings without external excitation. Modal mode turns into a tuned sine bank; sympathetic becomes a string drone.

behaviors.name: Polyphony note-stealing
behaviors.description: In 2- or 4-voice mode each STRUM allocates the oldest voice. Voices share DSP, so decay times shrink as polyphony rises. Tracks the last-played V/OCT for unpatched STRUM mode.

behaviors.name: Stereo ODD/EVEN spread
behaviors.description: ODD and EVEN carry the same note with complementary partial content. Panned hard L/R they produce a natural stereo image without any external effect.

behaviors.name: Chord mode (easter-egg model 4)
behaviors.description: Model 4 plays a 4-note diatonic chord per STRUM, with STRUCTURE choosing chord type. Effectively turns Rings into a chord-voice resonator.

patchIdeas.name: Karplus pluck sequencer
patchIdeas.patch: Sympathetic mode (green) · polyphony 1 · DAMPING ~2 o'clock · sequencer CV → V/OCT · gate → STRUM · BRIGHTNESS attenuverter modulated by envelope for evolving timbre · ODD/EVEN panned for stereo
patchIdeas.diagram:
Rings:
* Resonator: Sympathetic (green)
* Polyphony: 1
* Damping: ~2 o'clock

- Sequencer (Pitch) p> Rings (V/OCT)
- Sequencer (Gate) t> Rings (STRUM)
- Envelope (Out) >> Rings (BRIGHTNESS CV)
- Rings (ODD) -> Mixer (L)
- Rings (EVEN) -> Mixer (R)

patchIdeas.name: Bell model drone
patchIdeas.patch: Modal mode (orange) · DAMPING full CW (self-oscillation) · STRUCTURE swept slowly by LFO for inharmonicity · no STRUM patched · POSITION modulated by second LFO for shifting partials · ODD into reverb for infinite bell drones
patchIdeas.diagram:
Rings:
* Resonator: Modal (orange)
* Damping: full CW // self-oscillation

- LFO 1 (Out) >> Rings (STRUCTURE CV)
- LFO 2 (Out) >> Rings (POSITION CV)
- Rings (ODD) -> Reverb (In) -> Output (In)

patchIdeas.name: Drum resonator body
patchIdeas.patch: Any model · external drum loop into IN · breaks internal exciter normalling · V/OCT sequenced to pitch the resonance per beat · DAMPING short (0.5–1s) so the drum body doesn't smear · Rings sings the loop back as tuned resonant percussion
patchIdeas.diagram:
Rings:
* Resonator: Modal or Inharmonic
* Damping: short

- Drum Loop (Out) -> Rings (IN)
- Sequencer (Pitch) p> Rings (V/OCT)
- Rings (ODD) -> Mixer (In 1)
- Rings (EVEN) -> Mixer (In 2)

patchIdeas.name: No-strum pitch-triggered lead
patchIdeas.patch: Sympathetic mode · STRUM unpatched · Rings auto-triggers on V/OCT jumps · sequencer with legato rests keeps some notes tied without re-plucking · produces guitar-like hammer-on phrasing
patchIdeas.diagram:
Rings:
* Resonator: Sympathetic
* STRUM: unpatched // auto-trigger on V/OCT change

- Sequencer (Pitch) p> Rings (V/OCT)
- Rings (ODD) -> Delay (In) -> Output (In)

patchIdeas.name: Inharmonic FM string
patchIdeas.patch: Inharmonic mode (red) · STRUCTURE ~3 o'clock (high stiffness) · DAMPING ~1 o'clock · envelope into BRIGHTNESS for plucky attack · LFO into POSITION for bell-like shimmer · single-voice polyphony for longest decay
patchIdeas.diagram:
Rings:
* Resonator: Inharmonic (red)
* Structure: ~3 o'clock
* Polyphony: 1

- Sequencer (Pitch) p> Rings (V/OCT)
- Sequencer (Gate) t> Rings (STRUM)
- Envelope (Out) >> Rings (BRIGHTNESS CV)
- LFO (Out) >> Rings (POSITION CV)
- Rings (ODD) -> Mixer (L)
- Rings (EVEN) -> Mixer (R)

patchIdeas.name: Polyphonic piano chords
patchIdeas.patch: Sympathetic mode · polyphony 4 · sequencer outputs arpeggiated V/OCT · gate on every step strums each note into its own voice · DAMPING ~2 o'clock for piano decay · ODD/EVEN into chorus for piano body
patchIdeas.diagram:
Rings:
* Resonator: Sympathetic
* Polyphony: 4
* Damping: ~2 o'clock

- Sequencer (Arp CV) p> Rings (V/OCT)
- Clock (Out) t> Rings (STRUM)
- Rings (ODD) -> Chorus (L)
- Rings (EVEN) -> Chorus (R)
- Chorus (Out L/R) -> Output (L/R)

patchIdeas.name: External feedback shimmer
patchIdeas.patch: Modal mode · Rings ODD → reverb → back into IN via attenuator (set at ~10 o'clock to avoid runaway) · STRUM from slow clock · infinite recursive bell shimmer that tracks V/OCT
patchIdeas.diagram:
Rings:
* Resonator: Modal

- Clock (Out) t> Rings (STRUM)
- Sequencer (Pitch) p> Rings (V/OCT)
- Rings (ODD) -> Reverb (In)
- Reverb (Out) -> Attenuator (In) // ~10 o'clock, avoid runaway
- Attenuator (Out) -> Rings (IN) // feedback excitation
- Rings (EVEN) -> Output (In)

patchIdeas.name: Rings as stereo reverb
patchIdeas.patch: Modal mode · DAMPING near max (long tail) · BRIGHTNESS ~2 o'clock · external audio into IN · V/OCT fixed at 0V · Rings acts as a tuneable resonant reverb with ODD/EVEN as stereo outputs
patchIdeas.diagram:
Rings:
* Resonator: Modal
* Damping: near max
* V/OCT: 0V fixed

- External Audio (Out) -> Rings (IN)
- Rings (ODD) -> Mixer (L)
- Rings (EVEN) -> Mixer (R)
